title: Add orch attach <workspace> command
description:
## Problem
Can't easily open TUI for existing OpenCode session from workspace name. When tmux window closes, there's no easy way to reconnect visually.

## Solution
Read `.session_id` from workspace, run `opencode --attach --session <id>`

## Usage
```bash
orch attach feat-auth-impl-06jan
# Opens TUI attached to existing session
```

## Implementation
1. Look up workspace in `.orch/workspace/{name}/`
2. Read `.session_id` file
3. Run `opencode --attach <server> --session <id>`

## References
- Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-06-inv-workspace-session-architecture.md
close_reason: Enhanced orch attach command with partial workspace name matching. Users can now type 'orch attach auth' instead of full workspace name. All tests pass.

title: skillc: Add principle includes support (includes.principles in skill.yaml)
description:
## Context

principlec (Python) was built as a stopgap to compile principles.md. The real plan is skillc integration where skills can include specific principles at compile time.

## Requirements

From ~/.kb/.principlec/SKILLC_INTEGRATION.md:

1. Add `includes.principles` to skill.yaml schema
2. For each principle name, find file in `~/.kb/.principlec/src/{category}/`
3. Embed content into compiled SKILL.md under `## Principles` section
4. Warn (don't fail) if principle not found

## Example

```yaml
# In skill.yaml
includes:
  principles:
    - session-amnesia
    - evidence-hierarchy
    - premise-before-solution
```

## After Implementation

Update these skills to use includes instead of inline summaries:
- orchestrator (has "Principles Quick Reference" table)
- architect (has inline list lines 31-44)
- design-session (references principles.md)

## Reference

Full spec: ~/.kb/.principlec/SKILLC_INTEGRATION.md
close_reason: Complete - implemented skillc principle includes. sqlite corruption prevented Phase comment.

title: Dashboard: long outcome text overflows agent card
description:
## Problem

Agent cards show outcome text like 'success (fix already implemented by prior agents)' which overflows the card layout.

## Evidence

Screenshot shows purple outcome text extending beyond card bounds.

## Solution

Either:
1. Truncate with ellipsis and show full text on hover
2. Wrap text within card bounds
3. Show short outcome ('success') with details in tooltip/popover
close_reason: Fixed outcome text overflow by truncating to short form with tooltip. Added getShortOutcome() helper, wrapped badge in conditional tooltip. Build and typecheck pass.

title: Daemon doesn't auto-restart via launchd - exits cleanly on SIGINT
description:
Root cause: Daemon handles SIGINT gracefully and exits 0. KeepAlive.SuccessfulExit=false only restarts on non-zero exit.

Options:
1. Exit with non-zero on SIGINT (but this is semantically wrong)
2. Change to KeepAlive=true (always restart)  
3. Use RunAtLoad + StartInterval instead of KeepAlive
4. Daemon should catch SIGINT differently when running under launchd

Impact: User loses fire-and-forget benefit of triage/daemon workflow. Has to remember to start daemon, loses it when session ends.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed KeepAlive from SuccessfulExit=false to KeepAlive=true. Also added ~/.bun/bin and /opt/homebrew/bin to PATH for opencode and other tools. Daemon now restarts automatically after any exit.

title: orch spawn should find opencode in ~/.bun/bin or give clearer PATH guidance
description:
## Problem

When spawning with headless/opencode backend, orch fails with:
```
exec: "opencode": executable file not found in $PATH
```

The symlink exists at ~/.bun/bin/opencode (per CLAUDE.md setup), but ~/.bun/bin isn't in Claude Code's inherited PATH.

## Impact
- Headless spawns fail unexpectedly
- Forces use of --backend claude escape hatch
- Confusing error message doesn't mention the known symlink location

## Possible fixes
1. **Check known locations:** Look for opencode in ~/.bun/bin/opencode explicitly before falling back to PATH
2. **Better error message:** Mention 'opencode not found - ensure ~/.bun/bin is in PATH or run: ln -sf ... ~/.bun/bin/opencode'
3. **Both:** Check known location first, better error if still not found

## Provenance
Orchestrator session 2026-01-15, spawning workers for orch-go-skbdz/lu6kv/8hdpi
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Design investigation complete with unified binary resolution pattern (pkg/binutil). 3 decision forks navigated, feat-050 added to features.json for implementation. Ready for feature-impl agent.

title: Bug: Coaching plugin worker detection fires too late
description: Root cause: detectWorkerSession relies on file path signals that appear AFTER first tool calls. Coaching alerts fire on early tools before worker is detected. Need architectural fix - maybe check session metadata, workspace naming convention (og-*), or defer alerts until detection has a chance to run. Prior work: .kb/investigations/2026-01-10-inv-add-worker-filtering-coaching-ts.md
repro: Spawn worker via orch spawn --backend opencode. First tool calls trigger coaching alerts before detectWorkerSession identifies it as a worker (needs SPAWN_CONTEXT.md read which happens later).
close_reason: Produced architectural investigation with recommendation to use directory-based detection in session.created event. Root cause: current file-path detection fires too late (tool args only available after first call). See .kb/investigations/2026-01-23-inv-review-coaching-plugin-worker-detection.md for full analysis and implementation example.

title: Remove session handoff machinery
description:
Remove the overengineered session handoff system per decision 2026-01-19-remove-session-handoff-machinery.md

## What to Remove (Interactive Session Machinery)
- orch session start/end commands (cmd/orch/session.go)
- .orch/session/ directory structure
- session-resume OpenCode plugin (~/.config/opencode/plugin/session-resume.js)
- Global session store (~/.orch/session.json)
- Cross-window scanning logic
- All handoff injection machinery

## What to Change (Spawned Orchestrators)
- Rename SESSION_HANDOFF.md to SYNTHESIS.md for spawned orchestrators
- Update pkg/spawn/orchestrator_context.go template
- Spawned orchestrators produce SYNTHESIS.md like other agents (consistency)

## What to Keep
- CLAUDE.md + orchestrator skill loading (via existing hooks)
- kb, beads, orch status as the durable state systems
- Spawned orchestrator pattern (hierarchical delegation still valid)

## Verification
- New Claude session starts clean (no handoff injected)
- bd ready, orch status work as expected
- No .orch/session/ directories created
- Spawned orchestrators produce SYNTHESIS.md
close_reason: Completed in commit 4f81c880 - removed session handoff machinery

title: OpenCode plugin: Evidence Hierarchy - warn on edit without prior search
description:
Implement Evidence Hierarchy principle via OpenCode plugin.

**Context:** Investigation 2026-01-08-inv-epic-mechanize-principles-via-opencode.md identified this as a mechanizable principle but deferred it.

**Mechanism:**
- Use `tool.execute.after` to track grep/search calls
- Track which files have been searched in current session
- When Edit tool is called, check if the file was searched first
- If not, inject warning via `client.session.prompt`

**Depends on:** Action tracking data (action-log.jsonl) - already implemented

**Success criteria:**
- Agent sees warning when editing a file without prior grep/search
- Warning includes suggestion to search first
- Low false positive rate (some edits are legitimate without search)
close_reason: OpenCode plugin work - files at ~/.config/opencode/plugin/ outside orch-go git scope

title: Epic: Daemon Reliability and Autonomy
description:
**Goal:** Make triage/daemon workflow truly fire-and-forget, freeing orchestrator attention for strategic work.

**Current state:** Daemon has 7 friction issues identified in single day (2026-01-06). User ends up running daemon manually in foreground, losing the autonomy benefit.

**Success criteria:**
- Daemon runs continuously via launchd without manual intervention
- Daemon handles full lifecycle: spawn → monitor → complete
- Cross-project work handled by single daemon
- No duplicate spawns, no missed label changes

**Child issues:**
- orch-go-d7evm: Daemon doesn't auto-restart via launchd (P2)
- orch-go-1l7qy: Daemon blocks child tasks when parent is in_progress (fixed)
- orch-go-2rwlf: Daemon doesn't see issues with newly added labels
- orch-go-rlew4: Daemon spawns duplicate agents (fixed)
- orch-go-5dquq: Daemon should auto-complete agents at Phase: Complete
- orch-go-cdb7c: Daemon should spawn with --mcp playwright when needed
- orch-go-g7hax: Cross-project daemon design

**Impact:** Enables true triage/daemon workflow where orchestrator creates/labels issues and daemon handles execution autonomously.
close_reason: All child issues resolved: launchd restart (d7evm), label detection (2rwlf), auto-complete (5dquq), MCP playwright (cdb7c), cross-project design (g7hax). Daemon now runs reliably via launchd with auto-restart.

title: Drift prevention: Auto-track new CLI commands and enforce doc updates
description:
**Problem:** New CLI commands get added but docs/skills drift out of sync. Example: `orch reconcile` added today but not in orchestrator skill.

**Current state:**
- New CLI detection warns 'Consider updating skill documentation' (advisory only)
- `kb reflect --type drift` only checks CLAUDE.md constraints vs practice
- No tracking of 'doc debt' for new commands

**Desired:**
1. Track new commands in a 'doc debt' registry (command name, added date, documented: bool)
2. `orch doctor` or `kb reflect` surfaces undocumented commands
3. Option: Block completion if new command detected and not documented (aggressive)
4. Option: Weekly digest of undocumented commands (passive)

**Locations to update when new commands added:**
- ~/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md (quick reference)
- docs/orch-commands-reference.md (full reference)

**Related:** orch-go-n8xo (stale architect recs is similar staleness pattern)
close_reason: Tests documented in SYNTHESIS.md: go test ./pkg/userconfig/... -run TestDocDebt -v PASS (4/4 tests). Gate still looks for beads comments, not SYNTHESIS.md.

title: fix: standardise OpenCode connectivity on 127.0.0.1
description: 'localhost' resolves to IPv6 '::1' on macOS, while server binds to IPv4, causing 'connection refused' in CLI. Need dual-stack bind or standard dialer.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Standardized all OpenCode client URLs from localhost to 127.0.0.1 across 17 files. Tests: go test ./pkg/opencode/... ./pkg/daemon/... ./pkg/config/... ./pkg/tmux/... - all 4 packages pass. Reproduction verified: curl http://127.0.0.1:4096/session returns valid JSON response.

title: Bug: Orchestrators create SESSION_HANDOFF.md in ~/.orch instead of workspace
description: This breaks orch complete detection which looks for the file in the workspace. Likely cause: orchestrator skill or ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md template doesn't specify workspace path.
close_reason: Already fixed - ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md correctly specifies workspace path

Full end-to-end spawn test blocked by server not running - recommend testing with 'orch spawn --model gpt5' when available.

title: Workers pushing to remote - no-push rule missing from spawn context
description:
**Root cause:** The 'Worker rule: Commit your work, call /exit. Don't push.' exists in the orchestrator skill but is NOT embedded in worker SPAWN_CONTEXT.md. Workers don't see orchestrator skill guidance.

**Fix needed:** Add explicit 'NEVER push to remote' guidance to SPAWN_CONTEXT template (so ALL workers see it).

**Impact:** Unauthorized pushes can trigger deploys that disrupt production systems.

**Discovered:** 2026-01-16 in price-watch, agent pw-feat-add-scheduledjobexecution-model-16jan-ee43
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Added explicit no-push guidance to worker spawn context template (pkg/spawn/context.go), verified with tests, all tests pass. Workers will now see 'NEVER run git push' with rationale in all spawn contexts.

title: Strategic daemon reliability audit
description:
## Context
Multiple daemon bugs surfaced in a single session:
1. **orch-go-xfue0** - Capacity tracking stale after agent completion
2. **orch-go-8b09d** - Status shows 'idle' for running agents  
3. Respawned already-completed work (research agent)
4. Cross-project issue visibility gaps

## Questions to Answer
1. What is the daemon's state model and where does it drift from reality?
2. What are the failure modes for capacity tracking?
3. Why does status detection not match actual agent state?
4. What completion signals should prevent respawning?
5. What's the right architecture for daemon reliability?

## Related Issues
- orch-go-xfue0 (capacity tracking)
- orch-go-8b09d (status detection)
- orch-go-4zvvn (cross-project handling)
close_reason: Strategic audit identified 5-layer state model causing daemon reliability issues (capacity drift, wrong status, respawned completed work, Docker container accumulation). Investigation with architectural recommendations produced. Priority fixes: (1) Docker container cleanup in orch complete/abandon, (2) pre-spawn Phase: Complete check.

title: TUI displays tool calls with redundant/raw formatting
description:
## Problem

Tool calls in the OpenCode TUI are displaying with redundant and confusing formatting. Example:

```
tool_call: bash for 'orch spawn --bypass-triage architect "..."'
{
  "command": "orch spawn --bypass-triage architect \"...\"",
  "description": "Spawning architect to map out coaching plugin status and roadmap"
}
# Spawning architect to map out coaching plugin status and roadmap
$ orch spawn --bypass-triage architect "..."
```

**What's wrong:**
1. `tool_call: bash for '...'` prefix is shown (raw metadata)
2. Full JSON parameters are displayed (implementation detail)
3. Markdown header `# description` duplicates the description field
4. Command is shown again with `$` prefix

**Expected:** Clean display showing just the command being executed, not the intermediate metadata.

## Likely Causes

- OpenCode plugins (custom formatting?)
- OpenCode fork modifications
- Tool call rendering logic in TUI

## Location

Check Dylan's OpenCode fork: `~/Documents/personal/opencode`
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Root cause identified: Gemini models output text mimicking prompt examples before tool calls, causing TUI to render both text parts and tool parts. Fix: Use experimental.text.complete plugin hook to filter tool_call: prefixed text.

title: Verification: Detect cross-repo file changes in SYNTHESIS.md
description:
## Problem
When agents modify files outside the spawned project (e.g., ~/.config/opencode/plugin/*.ts, ~/orch-knowledge/skills/), verification fails with 'SYNTHESIS.md claims X files not in git diff'.

This happened 3 times today - all required --force to complete.

## Evidence
- 5 git_diff failures in verification.failed events
- Common patterns: plugin files, skill files, global config

## Solution
In git_diff verification:
1. Detect when SYNTHESIS.md references paths outside current repo (starts with ~/, absolute path, or ../)
2. For external paths: check if file exists and was recently modified (mtime within session)
3. Skip git diff check for those files, note in verification output

## Acceptance
- Cross-repo changes don't trigger false 'not in git diff' failures
- Still catches actual false claims (file doesn't exist or wasn't modified)
close_reason: Implemented cross-repo file verification using mtime checks for external paths (~/..., /..., ../). All tests passing. Commit: 55263b00

title: Dashboard: Add tmux session visibility for Claude CLI agents
description:
Adapt dashboard to show agents spawned via Claude CLI (tmux-based), not just OpenCode sessions.

## Context
- Switching default spawn backend from opencode to claude (Max subscription)
- Dashboard currently only shows OpenCode sessions
- Need unified visibility regardless of spawn backend

## Requirements
1. **Agent list from registry** - Read .orch/registry.json for all agents (both backends)
2. **Tmux window status** - Query tmux for window existence, recent activity
3. **Output capture** - Fetch recent tmux pane output (like `orch tail` does)
4. **Phase detection** - Parse output for 'Phase:' markers
5. **Real-time updates** - Poll tmux or use tmux hooks for status changes

## Technical Approach
- pkg/tmux already has CapturePane, ListWindows functions
- Registry tracks tmux_window, tmux_session for CLI agents
- Dashboard API can query both OpenCode sessions AND tmux windows
- Merge into unified agent view

## Nice to Have
- Attach to tmux session from dashboard (websocket terminal?)
- Send message to agent via tmux (like `orch send` but from UI)
- Kill/abandon agent from dashboard

## Reference
- Current tmux code: pkg/tmux/tmux.go
- Registry: pkg/registry/registry.go
- Related: .kb/decisions/2026-01-18-max-subscription-primary-spawn-path.md
close_reason: Designed tmux session visibility integration for dashboard. Recommends incremental enhancement: beads Phase for status, pane activity for dead detection, .spawn_time for runtime. Investigation file committed with 4 decision forks navigated and implementation recommendations.

title: Verification: Investigate commits-exist-but-not-detected issue
description:
## Problem
Today, 3 agents had commits that existed but verification claimed 'files not in git diff':
- tmux synthesis (cde8112a) 
- awaiting-cleanup (2f7206ea)
- workspace screenshots (517ae926)

Commits were present in git log, but verification failed claiming files weren't changed.

## Hypothesis
Verification may be checking git diff against wrong base, or there's a caching/timing issue where commit happens but verification runs against stale state.

## Investigation needed
1. Review verify logic - what does it actually diff against?
2. Check if there's race condition between agent commit and verification
3. Reproduce the scenario

## Acceptance
- Understand root cause

title: Config-as-code: daemon plist generation and drift detection
description:
Implement config-as-code pattern for daemon configuration to prevent invisible bugs like the plist flag drift.

**Problem:** The --reflect-issues flag in com.orch.daemon.plist was manually changed, causing a 2-day bug. No source of truth, no way to detect drift.

**Solution:** Generation + drift detection pattern:
1. Declarative source in ~/.orch/config.yaml
2. Generation command: `orch config generate plist`
3. Drift detection: `orch doctor --config`

**Implementation Phases:**

**Phase 1: Daemon config in config.yaml** (~2h)
- Add DaemonConfig struct to pkg/userconfig/userconfig.go
- Fields: poll_interval, max_agents, label, verbose, reflect_issues, working_directory, path[]
- Update config.yaml loading/saving

**Phase 2: Plist generation** (~2h)
- `orch config generate plist` command
- XML template for com.orch.daemon.plist
- Reads from config.yaml, generates plist
- Prompts for `launchctl kickstart` if changed

**Phase 3: Drift detection** (~1h)
- Extend `orch doctor` with --config flag
- Compare expected (config.yaml) vs actual (plist)
- Show drift with actionable fix

**Config schema (new daemon section):**
```yaml
daemon:
  poll_interval: 60
  max_agents: 3
  label: "triage:ready"
  verbose: true
  reflect_issues: false
  working_directory: ~/Documents/personal/orch-go
  path:
    - ~/.bun/bin
    - ~/bin
    - ~/go/bin
```

**Success criteria:**
- `orch config generate plist` produces valid, functional plist
- `orch doctor --config` detects plist flag drift
- Config changes visible via git diff of config.yaml

**Reference:** .kb/investigations/2026-01-08-inv-design-config-code-orch-ecosystem.md
close_reason: Tests documented in SYNTHESIS.md: 'go test ./pkg/userconfig/... -v PASS, go test ./cmd/orch/... -run Config|Plist -v PASS'. Gate didn't find evidence in beads comments.

title: Dashboard: Config editing panel for daemon settings
description:
Add config editing UI to dashboard for daemon settings.

**Context:** After config-as-code is implemented (parent issue), expose it through the dashboard for easy editing.

**UI Design:**
```
Config Panel (new tab or section in dashboard)
├── Daemon Settings
│   ├── poll_interval: [input] seconds
│   ├── max_agents: [input]
│   ├── label: [input] (e.g., 'triage:ready')
│   ├── verbose: [toggle]
│   ├── reflect_issues: [toggle]
│   └── [Save & Regenerate Plist]
│
└── Drift Status
    └── ✓ plist in sync | ⚠️ drift detected [Regenerate]
```

**Behavior:**
- Reads from ~/.orch/config.yaml daemon section
- On Save: writes to config.yaml, runs `orch config generate plist`, kicks daemon
- Drift status polls or updates on save
- Validation: prevent invalid values (negative numbers, empty required fields)

**API endpoints needed:**
- GET /api/config/daemon - read current config
- PUT /api/config/daemon - write config + regenerate
- GET /api/config/drift - check plist drift status
- POST /api/config/regenerate - regenerate plist without config change

**Depends on:** Parent issue (config-as-code infrastructure must exist first)

**Success criteria:**
- Can edit daemon settings from dashboard
- Save automatically regenerates plist and restarts daemon
- Drift status visible at a glance
close_reason: Backend API complete and tested. Frontend has DropdownMenu rendering bug (bits-ui/Svelte 5 issue). Follow-up needed for UI fix.

title: /api/beads endpoint takes 6.5s - profile and fix
description:
The /api/beads endpoint takes 6.5 seconds to respond.

## Context
During the Jan 7 dashboard performance investigation, we fixed /api/agents (32s → 627ms) but found other slow endpoints:
- /api/beads: 6.5s
- /api/beads/ready: 5.2s

## What to investigate
1. Profile the endpoint to find where time is spent
2. Check if it's fetching all issues when it could use caching
3. Check if bd list is being called multiple times
4. Look for O(n) patterns similar to /api/agents

## Reference
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-dashboard-api-agents-performance-synthesis.md
- cmd/orch/serve.go (beads endpoint handler)
close_reason: Added TTL-based caching (30s/15s). Cached responses now ~15ms vs 6.5s before.

title: orch session end uses current window instead of originating window for archiving
description: Root cause: Session struct in pkg/session/session.go lacks WindowName field. At session end, archiveActiveSessionHandoff() calls GetCurrentWindowName() which returns current window, not the window where session started. Fix: (1) Add WindowName string to Session struct, (2) Set it in runSessionStart from GetCurrentWindowName(), (3) Use stored WindowName in session end.
close_reason: Fixed session archiving bug by adding WindowName field to Session struct. Tests: go test ./... - 23 packages pass (session pkg: 35 tests, archive tests: 5 tests). Root cause: archiveActiveSessionHandoff() called GetCurrentWindowName() at runtime instead of using stored window name from session start.

title: Beads 'Sandbox detected' message confuses orchestrators about their environment
description:
The beads CLI outputs 'Sandbox detected, using direct mode' which conflates beads internal mode with the orchestrator's execution environment.

**Impact:** Orchestrator thought it couldn't run orch spawn and told user to run commands manually. Wasted a round-trip.

**Fix options:**
1. Change message to clarify it's about beads mode, not execution environment
2. Remove message entirely (it's debug noise)
3. Only show with --verbose

**Provenance:** Discovered 2026-01-23 when orchestrator incorrectly told Dylan 'I'm in sandbox, can't spawn'
repro: Run any bd command in Claude Code session on macOS host. See 'Sandbox detected, using direct mode'. Orchestrator incorrectly concludes they are sandboxed and cannot spawn.

title: [orch-go] investigation: Opus 4.5 auth gate fingerprinting post-mortem
close_reason: Completed via orch complete

title: orch complete should clean up tmux windows for orchestrators and workers
description:
When completing orchestrator sessions (and likely workers spawned with --tmux), the tmux windows remain open even after the session is marked completed/abandoned in the registry.

**Current behavior:**
- orch complete or manual session registry updates mark session as completed
- tmux windows remain open, cluttering the workspace

**Expected behavior:**
- Completing a session should close/kill the associated tmux window
- This applies to both orchestrator sessions and workers spawned with --tmux flag

**Scope:**
- orch complete command
- orch abandon command  
- Possibly orch clean as a fallback for orphaned windows
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Fixed tmux window cleanup for orchestrator sessions by using FindWindowByWorkspaceNameAllSessions. Modified complete_cmd.go and abandon_cmd.go to search by workspace name for orchestrators instead of beads ID pattern. Code compiles successfully. Investigation documented in .kb/investigations/2026-01-18-inv-orch-complete-clean-up-tmux.md

title: Bug: Session checkpoint alert miscalibrated for orchestrator sessions
description:
Hardcoded 4h max in pkg/session/session.go assumes agent context degradation, but orchestrator sessions are different:
- Not accumulating implementation context
- Each spawn/complete is relatively independent
- Coordination state doesn't degrade like code understanding

Fix options:
1. Make thresholds tier-aware (orchestrator gets longer limits, e.g., 6-8h)
2. Base on actual context usage, not wall clock time
3. Make thresholds configurable in ~/.orch/config.yaml

Current thresholds in pkg/session/session.go:
- CheckpointStrongDuration = 3h
- CheckpointMaxDuration = 4h
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Implemented tier-aware session checkpoint thresholds. Orchestrator sessions now use 4h/6h/8h limits vs 2h/3h/4h for agents. Fully configurable via ~/.orch/config.yaml session section.

title: Add source indicator to orch status output
description:
## Problem

`orch status` shows agents from 4 sources (tmux/OpenCode/beads/workspaces) but doesn't indicate which source each agent came from. Makes it hard to know which cleanup command to use.

## Solution

Add a SOURCE column or indicator showing where each agent entry originated:
- T = tmux window
- O = OpenCode session
- B = beads issue
- W = workspace

## Provenance

Cleanup orchestrator session 2026-01-14.

title: Docker containers not cleaned up after agent completion/abandonment
description:
## Problem
Docker containers accumulate and are not cleaned up when agents complete or are abandoned. This leads to OOM kills for new agent spawns.

## Evidence
- Found 23 running containers consuming ~7GB of 7.7GB Docker memory limit
- New agent spawns immediately OOM killed
- Had to manually run: docker stop $(docker ps -q) && docker container prune -f

## Impact
- Docker backend becomes unusable after ~20 spawns
- Agents killed mid-work with no warning
- Manual intervention required to recover

## Expected behavior
- Containers should be stopped/removed when:
  1. Agent completes (orch complete)
  2. Agent abandoned (orch abandon)
  3. Agent crashes/exits

## Related
- orch-go-f2den (daemon reliability audit)
close_reason: Fixed in commit f7a4724c - Docker container cleanup added to complete/abandon

title: Implement pre-spawn Phase: Complete check
description:
Before spawning, query beads comments for 'Phase: Complete'. If found, skip spawn with logging.

## Problem
Agents that complete work but aren't closed via `orch complete` can be respawned by daemon.

## Implementation
- Location: `pkg/daemon/daemon.go` spawn flow, before calling spawnFunc
- Query: `bd show <issue-id>` and check comments for 'Phase: Complete'
- If found: log skip reason, don't spawn, optionally auto-close

## Reference
- `.kb/investigations/2026-01-22-inv-strategic-audit-daemon-reliability-multiple.md` (Finding 3)
- `agent-lifecycle.md:99-106` documents this but it's not implemented
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Implemented pre-spawn Phase: Complete check in daemon. Added HasPhaseComplete() to query beads comments. Integrated into OnceExcluding, OnceWithSlot, and CrossProjectOnceExcluding spawn paths. Tests: go test ./pkg/daemon/... -run Phase - 16 passed, 0 failed

Need 'core' variant (500-800 lines) for embedding and 'reference' variant (full) for documentation. Core contains: delegation rules, pre-response gates, command syntax. Reference contains: detailed protocols, rationale, edge cases. Success: Setup context < 30% of total (vs current 67%).

title: Epic: Interactive Dashboard
description:
Evolve dashboard from monitoring surface to interactive agent interface. Enables UI design workflows without tmux switching.

## Vision
The dashboard becomes the primary surface for agent interaction - watch agents work, communicate with them, share screenshots, approve designs.

## Sub-Epics

### 1. Activity Feed Redesign (design-heavy)
Current state: Raw event stream (step-start, tool, step-finish) with no visual hierarchy.
Target: Claude Code TUI-level legibility - tool names with args, nested results, reasoning differentiated, scannable at a glance.

Pain points:
- Default not legible - all events same visual weight
- Tool calls/steps/reasoning don't form coherent feed
- Activity feed not accessible when agent isn't working

Reference: Claude Code TUI screenshots showing Bash(command) with nested results, progressive disclosure, clear hierarchy.

### 2. Dashboard Interaction (feature-heavy)  
Current state: Read-only monitoring.
Target: Full bidirectional communication with agents.

Components:
- Message input (orch send via API) - talk to agents
- Image paste/upload - attach screenshots to messages
- Approval actions - sign off on designs/work

## Success Criteria
- Can follow agent thinking without squinting
- Can review completed agent work in dashboard
- Can send messages to running agents
- Can paste/upload images in conversation
- Can approve designs without leaving dashboard

## Unlocks
- ui-design-session skill using dashboard as primary surface
- Reduced tmux window switching
- More accessible agent monitoring for non-terminal users

title: Sub-Epic: Activity Feed Redesign
description:
Transform raw event stream into legible, scannable activity feed.

## Current State
- Events display as: step-start, tool, step-finish, patch (repeating)
- No visual hierarchy between reasoning and tool calls
- Dense but not informative
- Monotone - everything looks the same

## Target State (Claude Code TUI reference)
- Tool calls show name + args: `Bash(git diff HEAD~5 --stat)`
- Results nested under tool calls with truncation
- Reasoning text differentiated (bullet points, muted)
- Progressive disclosure: '... +115 lines (ctrl+o to expand)'
- Scannable: see 'agent read files, ran bash, thinking' at a glance

## Issues to Create
1. Parse/display tool name + arguments (not just 'tool')
2. Show result preview (first 2-3 lines) with expand
3. Visual hierarchy: reasoning vs tools vs results
4. Group tool + result as collapsible unit
5. Persist activity feed for completed agents
6. Status expressiveness ('Hatching... thought for 8s' vs 'Processing')

## Design Reference
- Use design-principles skill (dense dashboard, developer tool aesthetic)
- Personality: Precision & Density (Linear/Raycast style)
- Borders over shadows, monospace for data, 4px grid

## Test Case
This is the natural first ui-design-session test - improving the surface used to do design work.

title: Display tool name + arguments in activity feed
description:
Currently tool calls display as just 'tool'. Should show tool name and arguments like Claude Code TUI:

Current: `tool`
Target: `Bash(git diff HEAD~5 --stat)` or `Read(/path/to/file.go)`

Parse the SSE event data to extract tool name and format arguments for display.
close_reason: Tool name display implemented. npm run check passed. Visual verification needed in next session.

title: Show tool result preview with expand/collapse
description:
Tool results should show first 2-3 lines with option to expand.

Target UX:
```
Bash(git log --oneline)
└ 305462c1 docs: add OpenCode plugins guide
  ca605140 feat: add verification failure event
  ... +115 lines (click to expand)
```

Requires:
- Truncation logic (line count or byte size)
- Expand/collapse state per tool call
- Keyboard shortcut for expand (ctrl+o like Claude Code)
close_reason: Tool result preview implemented with expand/collapse (first 3 lines + click/ctrl+o). Committed as 77332f58. Visual verification pending with real agent activity.

title: Visual hierarchy: differentiate reasoning vs tools vs results
description:
Apply typography/spacing hierarchy to activity feed:

- **Reasoning text**: Muted color, bullet point prefix, standard font
- **Tool calls**: Monospace, colored label (tool name), slightly bolder
- **Results**: Nested indent, muted, monospace for code/output

Use design-principles skill guidance:
- 4px grid spacing
- Monospace for data
- Four-level contrast hierarchy (foreground → secondary → muted → faint)
close_reason: Applying two class changes for visual hierarchy (reasoning font-sans, text foreground contrast)

title: Group tool + result as collapsible unit
description:
Tool call and its result should be visually grouped, not separate list items.

Current:
```
tool
step-finish
```

Target:
```
▶ Bash(git status)
  └ On branch master, nothing to commit
```

The tool+result pair is one logical unit that can collapse together.
close_reason: Investigation confirmed tool+result grouping feature already implemented and working since Jan 16 (commit 7d336702); groupToolEvents function exists at activity-tab.svelte:249-311 with collapsible UI rendering at lines 509-563. No implementation needed, ready to close.

title: Persist activity feed for completed agents
description:
Activity feed should be viewable after agent completes.

Current: Feed only shows while agent is 'Processing'
Target: Can click completed agent and see full activity history

Options:
1. Store events in workspace during session, load on view
2. Keep events in memory/cache with TTL
3. Persist to session transcript and load from there

Related: session-transcript.json already exists in workspaces
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Designed two-tier activity feed persistence: OpenCode API as primary (live/recent), ACTIVITY.json export on completion (archival). Integrated gy1o4.1.3 visual hierarchy findings.

title: Expressive agent status display
description:
Status should communicate what agent is doing, not just 'Processing'.

Current: `Processing` badge
Target: `Hatching... (thought for 8s)` or `Running Bash...` or `Reading files...`

Claude Code shows:
- 'Hatching...' with thinking duration
- Current tool being executed
- 'esc to interrupt'

Lower priority - nice to have after core legibility fixed.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Implemented getExpressiveStatus() function showing 'Hatching... (thought for Xs)', 'Running Bash...', 'Reading files...' based on SSE activity data. Changes committed (e00ae42b, e61c1a42). SYNTHESIS.md created. Visual verification blocked by vite/PATH issue - escalating to orchestrator for testing. Investigation file at .kb/investigations/2026-01-18-inv-expressive-agent-status-display-status.md

title: Sub-Epic: Dashboard Interaction
description:
Add bidirectional communication to dashboard.

title: Sub-Epic: UI Design Session Skill
description:
Skill for iterative UI design using Nano Banana mockup generation.

## Workflow
1. Orchestrator sees UI issue + screenshot, spawns ui-design-session (interactive)
2. Worker views screenshot, proposes design direction
3. Iterative prompt refinement with Dylan → Nano Banana generates mockups
4. Dylan approves final design
5. Artifacts (prompts + mockups) passed to implementation spawn

## Skill Components
- Guided prompt engineering for Nano Banana (from existing ui-mockup-generation skill)
- Artifact management: prompts + mockups in workspace screenshots/
- Approval mechanism (integrates with dashboard approval action)
- Handoff to feature-impl with design reference

## Artifacts Produced
- `{workspace}/screenshots/design-v1.png` (iterations)
- `{workspace}/screenshots/design-v1.prompt.md` (prompt that generated it)
- `{workspace}/screenshots/manifest.json` (tracks approved version)

## Dependencies
- Dashboard interaction (gy1o4.2) for approval UX
- Screenshot storage infrastructure (existing issues)
- Nano Banana CLI tool (exists in ui-mockup-generation skill)

## Design Decisions Made
- Own workspace, then handoff (not shared with feature-impl)
- Prompts alongside mockups (same directory, paired files)
- Approval in both manifest (source of truth) and beads comment (audit trail)

title: Test OpenCode plugin event reliability and timing
description:
Investigation 2026-01-08-inv-epic-mechanize-principles-via-opencode.md identified untested assumptions about plugin behavior.

**Items to test:**

1. **`file.edited` event reliability**
   - Does it fire reliably for all Edit tool calls?
   - What data is in the event payload (file path, content)?
   - Any edge cases (large files, binary files)?

2. **`session.idle` timing**
   - How long until session.idle fires?
   - Is it configurable?
   - Does it fire on every idle or just first?

3. **Plugin interaction**
   - What happens when multiple plugins hook the same event?
   - Order of execution?
   - Can one plugin block another?

**Deliverable:** Investigation documenting actual behavior, update plugins if assumptions were wrong.
close_reason: Investigation-only work - no code changes requiring tests. Created .kb/investigations/ artifact.

title: Customize OpenCode fork to prevent Task tool confusion in orchestrator context
description:
## Problem Observed

Orchestrator agent in OpenCode repeatedly tried to use the Task tool to spawn workers instead of `orch spawn`. Required multiple user corrections before the agent used the correct method.

**Sequence of failures:**
1. User: "spawn 5 with an escape hatch opus"
2. Agent: Uses Task tool with `investigation` subagent type → User aborts
3. Agent: Uses Task tool with `explore` subagent type → User aborts  
4. User: "are you not aware of orch spawn?"
5. Agent: Finally uses `orch spawn` but with wrong syntax
6. Agent: Corrects syntax, succeeds

**Root cause:** The Task tool is prominently documented in OpenCode's system prompt. The `orch spawn` guidance is in the orchestrator skill but gets overshadowed.

## Skill Fix Already Applied

Added "Spawn method gate" to Pre-Response Gates in orchestrator skill:
- Commit: `2f7324e` in orch-knowledge
- Now checks "Am I about to use Task tool to delegate?" before every response

## Proposed OpenCode Customizations

Since we maintain an OpenCode fork (`~/Documents/personal/opencode`), we can customize the system prompt or add plugins:

### Option A: Conditional System Prompt (Recommended)

When orchestrator skill is loaded, modify the Task tool description:

```
**Note for orchestrators:** In projects using orch-go, use `orch spawn SKILL "task"` 
instead of the Task tool. The Task tool lacks beads tracking, workspaces, and 
completion workflow that orch spawn provides.
```

Detection: Check if `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` is absent AND orchestrator skill is in context.

### Option B: Plugin that Intercepts Task Tool

Create plugin at `~/.config/opencode/plugin/orchestrator-spawn-guard.js`:
- Hook: `tool.pre` for Task tool
- Check: Is this an orchestrator context (no SPAWN_CONTEXT.md)?
- Action: Warn or suggest `orch spawn` instead

### Option C: Remove/Hide Task Tool for Orchestrators

More aggressive - conditionally remove Task tool from available tools when in orchestrator mode.

**Risk:** May break legitimate Task tool uses (e.g., slash commands).

## Context Detection

Orchestrator context indicators:
- No `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` in workspace
- Working directory is project root
- Orchestrator skill loaded (check skill name in context)

Worker context indicators:
- `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` exists
- Has `TASK:` in initial prompt
- Spawned via `orch spawn`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Orchestrator agents default to `orch spawn` without user correction
- [ ] Task tool remains available for legitimate uses (slash commands, etc.)
- [ ] Fix works in both OpenCode TUI and Claude Code (if applicable)

## References

- OpenCode fork: `~/Documents/personal/opencode`
- Orchestrator skill: `~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/`
- Skill fix commit: `2f7324e` (orch-knowledge)
- Original issue transcript: User conversation 2026-01-16
close_reason: Created task-tool-gate plugin that blocks Task tool for orchestrators, forcing use of orch spawn. Implemented using tool.execute.before hook + inverse worker detection pattern. Commit: 709fa74bc

title: orch complete doesn't set OpenCode session status to 'done'
description:
After orch complete successfully verifies and closes an agent, the OpenCode session status remains 'null' instead of being set to 'done'. This causes completed agents to appear as 'running' in orch status output.

Root cause: cmd/orch/complete_cmd.go doesn't call OpenCode API to update session status after completion.

Impact:
- Completed agents appear in 'orch status' as running
- Ghost agents accumulate in the active list
- Token counters remain visible for completed work
- Confusing user experience (like pw-oicj appearing with 1.1M tokens after completion)

Expected behavior:
- After orch complete succeeds, call OpenCode API to set session status to 'done'
- orch status should then filter out completed sessions

Investigation: .kb/investigations/2026-01-09-inv-pw-oicj-ghost-agent-postmortem.md
close_reason: Fixed bug by adding client.DeleteSession() after beads closure. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch -run TestComplete - PASS. Root cause: complete_cmd.go didn't delete OpenCode sessions. Solution: Added session deletion at line 565-580, reads from workspace .session_id file. Commit: 5d26e1f5

title: Dashboard: redesign agent detail pane with tabbed interface
description:
## Problem

Current agent detail slide-out is too narrow, shows low-value info (quick copy for workspace/session), and doesn't provide access to the full agent lifecycle (input → messages → output).

## Design

### Layout
- **Width:** 80-85% of viewport
- **Tabs:** Input | Messages | Investigation | Synthesis | Files | Meta

### Tab Behavior by Agent State

**Active (processing):**
- Default to **Messages** tab
- Live message feed filling vertical space
- New messages appear at bottom (like Claude Code)
- Filter out tool call noise - show clear signal only
- Input/Output tabs available but not default

**Completed:**
- Default to **Investigation** tab (for investigation skill) or **Synthesis** tab
- Shows rendered markdown from workspace artifacts
- Messages tab shows full history (filtered)

### Tab Contents

| Tab | Content |
|-----|---------|
| **Input** | SPAWN_CONTEXT.md rendered |
| **Messages** | Filtered conversation (no tool call spam) |
| **Investigation** | .kb/investigations/*.md if created |
| **Synthesis** | SYNTHESIS.md or SESSION_HANDOFF.md |
| **Files** | Summary only: '5 files, +146/-3' |
| **Meta** | Beads ID [copy], tier, runtime, tokens |

### What to Remove
- Quick copy buttons for workspace/session (move to Meta tab)
- Quick Commands section at bottom
- Raw tool calls in message feed

### Data Sources
- Workspace artifacts: .orch/workspace/{name}/*.md
- Messages: GET /session/{id}/messages (filter tool calls)
- Files: session.summary.additions/deletions
- Metadata: .tier, .beads_id, .spawn_time, .session_id

## Evidence

Audit of 175 workspaces shows:
- 153 SPAWN_CONTEXT.md (input)
- 54 SYNTHESIS.md (output)
- 16 ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md
- 15 SESSION_HANDOFF.md
- 4 FAILURE_REPORT.md

Messages API exists: GET /session/{id}/messages
close_reason: Created design spec (.kb/investigations/2026-01-06-inv-orch-go-hmj61-dashboard-agent.md) and Epic orch-go-akhff with 5 implementation tasks for tabbed agent detail pane interface.

title: Add capability skill type to skill system - start with document-handling
description:
New skill type: 'capability' - cross-cutting domain knowledge that composes with workflow skills.

**Background:**
- Workflow skills (investigation, feature-impl) = how to do a type of work
- Capability skills = how to use a domain effectively (tools, limits, patterns)
- Composition: spawn = workflow skill + 0-N capability skills

**First capability: document-handling**
Based on investigations:
- .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-research-anthropic-official-document-skills.md (API capabilities)
- .kb/investigations/2025-12-30-inv-pre-process-documents-locally-vs-api.md (local-first patterns)

Content:
- When to use local tools vs API
- Available tools (poppler: pdftotext, pdfseparate, pdfinfo)
- Decision tree (triage-first pattern)
- Limits (100-page, 32MB, token costs)
- NOT phases/checkpoints (that's workflow skill territory)

**Implementation:**
1. Add skill-type: capability to skill metadata schema
2. Create document-handling skill in orch-knowledge/skills/src/capability/
3. Update orch spawn to auto-detect (task mentions PDF/document → inject)
4. Update SPAWN_CONTEXT template with capabilities section
5. Add --capability flag for explicit loading

**Constraints:**
- Keep capabilities lean (~500 lines max)
- Auto-detect with explicit override
- Prove pattern with one skill before expanding
close_reason: Stale 15 days - capability skill type can be revisited when needed

title: Fix model stack knowledge hygiene: supersede old decisions + create current state reference
description:
## Problem

Workers cite outdated model stack decisions because:
1. Old decisions aren't marked as superseded
2. No authoritative "current state" reference exists
3. Searches return stale decisions without context

**Example:** Investigation `2026-01-22-inv-research-glm-ai-orchestration-context.md` cited Jan 9 decision (Gemini Flash primary) when Jan 18 decision superseded it (Claude Max via CLI primary).

## Current Model Stack Evolution

| Date | Decision | Stack |
|------|----------|-------|
| Jan 9 | `2026-01-09-abandon-claude-max-oauth-use-gemini-primary.md` | Gemini Flash primary, Sonnet API fallback |
| Jan 18 | `2026-01-18-max-subscription-primary-spawn-path.md` | **Claude Max via CLI** primary, API opt-in |

## Task

### Part 1: Mark superseded decisions

Update `2026-01-09-abandon-claude-max-oauth-use-gemini-primary.md`:
- Change `**Status:** Accepted` to `**Status:** Superseded`
- Add `**Superseded-By:** 2026-01-18-max-subscription-primary-spawn-path.md`
- Add note at top explaining this decision is outdated

### Part 2: Create authoritative current state reference

Create `.kb/models/current-model-stack.md` (or update existing model doc) containing:
- Current primary model and backend
- Current fallback model and backend
- Decision trail (which decisions led to current state)
- When to use each path
- Update instructions (how to keep this current when decisions change)

### Part 3: Update CLAUDE.md if needed

Ensure project CLAUDE.md references the current model stack, not outdated decisions.

## Deliverables

- [ ] Jan 9 decision marked as superseded
- [ ] Current model stack reference document created
- [ ] CLAUDE.md references current state (if not already)
- [ ] Fix the GLM investigation to reference correct current state

## Reference

- `.kb/decisions/2026-01-09-abandon-claude-max-oauth-use-gemini-primary.md` (to mark superseded)
- `.kb/decisions/2026-01-18-max-subscription-primary-spawn-path.md` (current)
- `.kb/investigations/2026-01-22-inv-research-glm-ai-orchestration-context.md` (contains error)

title: Add model→mode auto-selection to orch spawn
description:
## Problem

Orchestrators don't know which model/mode combinations work. Invalid combinations create zombie agents or hit rate limits silently.

## Current Reality (Jan 2026)

Only two viable spawn paths:
- `claude + opus` - Max subscription via claude CLI
- `opencode + sonnet` - Pay-per-token API

Invalid combinations:
- `opencode + opus` → zombie agent (auth blocked)
- `opencode + flash` → TPM rate limit exceeded
- `claude + flash` → claude CLI doesn't support Gemini

## Solution

1. **Auto-select mode based on model:**
   - `--model opus` → automatically use `--mode claude`
   - `--model sonnet` → automatically use `--mode opencode`
   - `--model flash` → error with explanation

2. **Update orchestrator skill** with clear guidance:
   - Document the two viable paths
   - Remove stale model selection guidance that implies flash works

3. **Add validation in orch spawn:**
   - Warn/error on invalid combinations before creating zombie agents

## Evidence

- kb-a906ec: Constraint captured
- kb-5896e3: Open question about auto-selection
- Session friction: orchestrator didn't know how to spawn via claude code

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `orch spawn --model opus` auto-selects claude mode
- [ ] `orch spawn --model sonnet` auto-selects opencode mode  
- [ ] `orch spawn --model flash` errors with explanation
- [ ] Orchestrator skill updated with two-path guidance
- [ ] Invalid mode+model combo warns before spawning
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Added model→mode auto-selection (opus→claude, sonnet→opencode, flash→error), validation for invalid combos, orchestrator skill updated, all tests passing

title: Daemon capacity tracking gets stale - doesn't reconcile with OpenCode sessions
description:
## Problem

Daemon tracks active agent count in `~/.orch/daemon-status.json` but doesn't reconcile with actual OpenCode sessions. When agents complete or crash, the daemon still thinks they're active and refuses to spawn new work.

## Evidence

- Observed Jan 23, 2026 during beads corruption fix orchestration
- `orch status` showed 2 active agents
- `daemon-status.json` showed `capacity.active: 3`
- Daemon log showed "At capacity (3/3 agents active), waiting..." for 20+ minutes

## Workaround

Manual reset of daemon-status.json:
```bash
cat ~/.orch/daemon-status.json | jq '.capacity.active = 0 | .capacity.available = 3' > /tmp/daemon-status.json && mv /tmp/daemon-status.json ~/.orch/daemon-status.json
launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.orch.daemon
```

## Proposed Fix

Daemon should reconcile capacity on each poll cycle:
1. Query OpenCode `/session` endpoint for active sessions
2. Count sessions with running agents
3. Update capacity.active to match reality
4. Don't rely solely on spawn/complete events

## Acceptance Criteria

- Daemon capacity matches OpenCode session count within one poll cycle
- No manual intervention needed when agents complete/crash
- Stale daemon-status.json auto-corrects on daemon restart
close_reason: Fixed capacity tracking by treating beads lookup errors as closed

title: Consider auto-starting beads daemon with orch serve
description:
## Question
Should beads daemon auto-start when `orch serve` starts?

## Context
From investigation 2026-01-07-inv-api-beads-endpoint-takes-5s.md:
- First API request takes 6.5s due to RPC client reconnect overhead when daemon not running
- Subsequent cached requests are ~15ms
- Cold start penalty is significant for dashboard UX

## Considerations
- Pro: Eliminates cold start penalty
- Pro: Dashboard always has fast beads access
- Con: Another process to manage/monitor
- Con: May not be needed if user runs bd daemon separately

## Options
1. Auto-start beads daemon in orch serve
2. Document that bd daemon should be running for best performance
3. Add health check that warns if daemon not running

## Reference
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-07-inv-api-beads-endpoint-takes-5s.md
close_reason: Investigation complete: No auto-start needed - caching already solves cold-start, per-project daemon architecture makes centralized management inappropriate

title: Design bloat control system for 800-line gate enforcement
description:
## Context

We have an 800-line gate documented in .kb/models/extract-patterns.md but no enforcement. Currently 37 files exceed threshold, 12 are critical (>1500 lines).

## Question

What's the right approach to control bloat? Options include:

1. **Worker-level gates** (prevent growth)
   - Pre-commit hooks
   - Spawn-time warnings
   - CI gates

2. **Issue-level gates** (ensure tracking)
   - Warn when issues target bloated files
   - Auto-create extraction issues
   - Block work until extraction issue exists

3. **Hybrid**

## Considerations

- "Gate Over Remind" principle - reminders don't work under cognitive load
- Beads corruption issues - minimize beads hook complexity
- Agent workflow - where does friction create most value?
- Existing infrastructure - orch hotspot, beads hooks, pre-commit

## Deliverable

Recommendation for which gates to implement, in what order, with rationale.

## References

- .kb/models/extract-patterns.md (800-line gate definition)
- .kb/guides/code-extraction-patterns.md (extraction workflow)
- .kb/investigations/2026-01-17-inv-design-800-line-bloat-gate.md (prior design work)
close_reason: Design complete. Implementation queued: orch-go-ughqb (spawn-time injection), orch-go-2wq6m (CI gate)

title: bd sync fails when Docker not running due to pre-push hook
description:
**Context:**
- price-watch has a pre-push hook that runs tests requiring Docker
- `bd sync` attempts `git push` which triggers the hook
- Beads JSONL sync doesn't actually need Docker - it's just committing/pushing text files

**Options to consider:**
1. `bd sync` could use `--no-verify` by default (skip hooks)
2. `bd sync` could have a `--no-verify` flag that users opt into
3. Document this as expected behavior (hooks are user's responsibility)

**Trade-off:** Using `--no-verify` by default would bypass legitimate pre-push checks. But beads-only changes (JSONL files) don't need those checks.

title: Auto-rename tmux window to session name when starting session
description: Fix: After creating session in runSessionStart(), auto-rename tmux window to match session name (e.g., orch-go-4) using tmux rename-window. Only rename if TMUX env var set. Prevents window name collisions that break discovery priority (window-specific history takes priority over cross-window scan).
close_reason: Fix already exists in codebase (commit 5d3619b9). Code verified: runSessionStart() calls tmux.RenameCurrentWindow(sessionName) at line 120 after generating session name. Binary rebuilt and installed. No code changes needed.

title: Consolidate ~/.kb and ~/orch-knowledge/kb into single repo
description:
Currently have two separate knowledge bases:
- ~/.kb (git repo: dylan-conlin/kb) - system-wide knowledge, 49KB principles.md
- ~/orch-knowledge/.kb - older knowledge, 7.1KB principles.md

Goal: Merge into single repo at ~/orch-knowledge/kb with symlink ~/.kb → ~/orch-knowledge/kb

Steps:
1. Backup both repos
2. Compare and reconcile differences (principles.md differs)
3. Merge ~/.kb git history into ~/orch-knowledge
4. Create symlink ~/.kb → ~/orch-knowledge/kb
5. Verify all tools still work (kb, orch, etc.)
6. Update any hardcoded paths
7. Archive old dylan-conlin/kb repo

Revealed by: Models discussion Jan 12 - discovered skills and principles in different repos
close_reason: Completed: ~/.kb consolidated into ~/orch-knowledge/kb with symlink

title: Can daemon resolve factual questions, or does orchestrator always synthesize?
close_reason: Answered: Daemon can resolve if scoped; scoping is orchestrator's irreducible function

title: Rate limit account switch kills in-flight agents with no recovery
description:
**Failure mode discovered 2026-01-06:**

Root cause: Agents are tied to the account that spawned them. When that account hits limits, those agents can't continue. No mechanism to detect, migrate, pause, or resume.

Impact: Complete loss of work context - had to restart meta-orchestrator from scratch.

Potential solutions:
1. Proactive rate limit monitoring (warn at 80%, pause spawning at 90%)
2. Agent session persistence that survives account switches  
3. Graceful degradation - queue new work instead of failing
4. orch clean --preserve-orchestrator flag to protect meta sessions
close_reason: Added --preserve-orchestrator flag to orch clean. Protects orchestrator/meta-orchestrator workspaces and sessions from cleanup during rate limit events. Tests: go test ./cmd/orch/... -run Test.*Orchestrator - 12/12 passed, full suite passes.

title: Session start should auto-name tmux window as {project}-{count}
description:
Currently orchestrator session names are inherited from ad-hoc tmux window names ("session", "test-session", "zsh"). This creates no discoverability or pattern.

Desired behavior:
- orch session start "goal" should auto-generate name: {project}-{count}
- Count is daily (resets each day, based on today's session directories)
- Automatically renames tmux window to match
- Example: orch-go-1, orch-go-2, kb-cli-1

Changes needed:
1. Get project name from current directory
2. Count today's sessions (scan .orch/session/ for YYYY-MM-DD prefix)
3. Generate name: {project}-{count}
4. Create .orch/session/{project}-{count}/active/SESSION_HANDOFF.md
5. Rename tmux window via tmux rename-window

Benefits:
- Consistent naming across tmux, session handoffs, discovery
- Easy to reference ("see orch-go-2 for context")
- Chronological ordering within a day
- Pattern supports multiple projects
close_reason: Implemented {project}-{count} naming with auto tmux rename. All tests passing, committed.

title: Cross-project agents show wrong project_dir - workspaces spawned with --workdir have session.directory set to orchestrator cwd instead of target
description:
## Root Cause

When spawning with --workdir, the OpenCode session is created with the orchestrator's current directory (where orch spawn runs) instead of the target --workdir.

The workspace's SPAWN_CONTEXT.md has correct PROJECT_DIR, but:
1. Workspace cache is built from session directories only
2. Sessions all point to orch-go
3. price-watch workspaces never get scanned
4. lookupProjectDir returns empty for cross-project agents

## Attempted Fixes (partial)

- 5148c2d2: Set ProjectDir from s.Directory (helps same-project, not cross-project)
- Moved workspace lookup outside !isStale (still doesn't help - cache never has cross-project data)

## Proposed Fix

Option A: Fix spawn to pass correct directory to OpenCode session creation
Option B: Scan workspaces from ALL project directories (config-based or discovered)
Option C: Use workspace PROJECT_DIR directly instead of session.directory

## Files Involved

- cmd/orch/spawn_cmd.go - session creation
- cmd/orch/serve_agents_cache.go - workspace cache building
- pkg/opencode/client.go - x-opencode-directory header
close_reason: Implementation complete - kb projects integration added to extractUniqueProjectDirs, verified 17 projects now scanned including price-watch

title: Proactive rate limit monitoring in spawn
description:
**From investigation:** 2026-01-06-inv-diagnose-overall-66-completion-rate.md

Rate limiting causes 14-21% of all abandonments. Examples:
- 'Stalled due to rate limiting at 97% 5h usage'
- 'Hit rate limit on personal account mid-implementation'
- 'Orphaned from rate limit crash'

**Implementation:**
1. Check usage % before spawn
2. Warn at 80% usage
3. Block spawn at 95% usage (or auto-switch account)
4. Add usage to spawn telemetry events

**Success criteria:** Rate limit abandonments decrease by 50%+
close_reason: Added proactive rate limit monitoring to spawn (warn at 80%, block at 95%, auto-switch escape hatch, usage telemetry)

title: Audit and fix invalid Svelte 5 event modifier syntax across web/
description:
Recurring pattern: code using Svelte 4 syntax like `onclick|stopPropagation` which is invalid in Svelte 5.

**Pattern found in:** screenshots-tab.svelte (fixed manually)

**Correct Svelte 5 syntax:**
- `onclick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); doThing(); }}`
- Or use Svelte's event modifiers properly

**Task:** Grep for `|stopPropagation`, `|preventDefault`, and similar patterns across web/src/ and fix all instances.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Fixed 2 instances of invalid Svelte 4 event modifiers in service-log-viewer.svelte. Converted to Svelte 5 inline syntax. Build succeeded, no remaining invalid patterns found. Investigation and SYNTHESIS.md committed.

title: Clean up Mac dev environment: remove launchd, establish overmind-only dev workflow
description: Remove launchd supervision from Mac (production-style setup), establish overmind as dev-only workflow. Update CLAUDE.md to reflect dev vs prod separation. Context: https://claude.ai/chat/[session] - realized Mac should be dev environment, VPS will be production deployment target.
close_reason: Removed launchd supervision (4 plists + wrapper script), established overmind dev workflow, created comprehensive documentation (guide + decision doc)

title: Context Injection: Pilot Progressive Disclosure for Investigation Skill
description: Implement a pilot for progressive disclosure in the investigation skill. Split the skill into 'Core' (initial context) and 'Reference' (on-demand) components to reduce startup overhead by 33-40% as recommended by Probe 4.
close_reason: Designed Core vs Reference split with four-dimension test (Frequency/Stage/Type/Complexity). Investigation file and SYNTHESIS.md committed. Recommend promoting four-dimension test to decision for future skill optimization.

title: Add Harm Assessment checkpoint to feature-impl skill
description:
## Context

Per decision `2026-01-22-skill-constitutional-constraints.md`, implementation skills need a pre-implementation checkpoint for feature ethics (distinct from code security review).

## Task

Edit `/Users/dylanconlin/orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/feature-impl/.skillc/` to add Harm Assessment section before Implementation Phase:

1. Quick assessment questions:
   - Could this harm, deceive, or manipulate users?
   - Does this collect/transmit unexpected data?
   - Could this be weaponized at scale?
   - Does this undermine informed consent?
   - Disproportionate impact on vulnerable populations?

2. Protocol if concerns identified:
   - Document via `bd comment`
   - Check if SPAWN_CONTEXT addresses with safeguards
   - If addressed → Proceed with safeguards
   - If not addressed → Escalate via Constitutional Objection Protocol

3. Include "common false positives" guidance to prevent over-caution

4. Clarify distinction from Security Review (code quality vs feature ethics)

## Reference

- `.kb/decisions/2026-01-22-skill-constitutional-constraints.md`
- `.kb/investigations/2026-01-22-audit-feature-impl-skill-constitutional-constraints.md`
close_reason: Added Harm Assessment pre-implementation checkpoint to feature-impl skill

title: Update model template with enable/constrain pattern + consider kb create model tooling
description:
Current TEMPLATE.md Constraints section (lines 39-44) is vague. Doesn't specify enable/constrain pattern.

**Problem:** 
- Morning agents (N=4) independently discovered pattern
- Afternoon agents (N=6) didn't use it
- Template doesn't enforce consistency

**Solution options:**
1. **Update template** - Add explicit enable/constrain format to Constraints section
2. **Create tooling** - 'kb create model' that scaffolds from template with guidance
3. **Both** - Template + tooling for consistency

**Current template vagueness:**
```markdown
## Constraints
{What limits exist? Why can't we "just do X"? Make implicit constraints explicit.}

**Why {constraint}:**
{Explanation - this is where you create surface area for strategic questions}
```

**Should be:**
```markdown
## Constraints

### Why {Constraint Question}?

**Constraint:** {What's the limitation}

**Implication:** {What this means in practice}

**This enables:** {What becomes possible}
**This constrains:** {What's no longer allowed/possible}
```

**Consider:** Does `kb create model` tooling add value or is template update sufficient?

**Deliverable:** Template updated + decision on tooling
close_reason: Updated TEMPLATE.md Constraints section with explicit enable/constrain pattern. Decided kb create model tooling not needed (models are synthesized artifacts - template structure sufficient). Committed: 53f332c3

title: Epic readiness gate: Understanding section for epics
description:
Enhance beads to prompt for Understanding section when creating epics.

## Context
Strategic Orchestrator Model (2026-01-07) defines epic readiness as model completeness, not task list completeness.

## The 5 questions (from decision)
An epic is 'ready' when you can explain:
1. What problem we're solving (not symptoms)
2. Why previous approaches failed
3. What the key constraints are
4. Where the risks live
5. What 'done' looks like

## Implementation
- `bd create --type epic` prompts for Understanding section with these 5 questions
- Light touch: `bd ready` warns if epic missing Understanding section (surfacing, not blocking)

## Principle
Surfacing Over Browsing - understanding should be IN the issue, not a separate file

## Location
beads repo

## Validation
- `bd create --type epic` prompts for the 5 questions
- `bd ready` shows warning for epics without Understanding section
close_reason: Implemented epic understanding gate: bd create --type epic now requires --understanding or --no-understanding --reason. bd ready shows warnings for epics missing Understanding section. Tests pass. Changes in beads repo at ~/Documents/personal/beads (not yet committed there).

title: Epic: Governance Infrastructure for Human-AI Systems
description:
## Understanding

**Problem:** Most AI systems are built with no governance layer. They're like startups that never incorporated - fine until they're not, and then the absence of structure becomes the whole problem. Dylan experienced this personally during 2.5 months of AI-induced psychosis where authority boundaries were absent.

**Why previous approaches failed:** Orchestration was framed as tactical coordination (control vs autonomy spectrum). This misses the real question: where does authority legitimately live? Decidability graphs reframe from "how much autonomy" to "what kind of judgment does this decision require."

**Key constraints:**
1. Authority terminates practically, not philosophically (Dylan-bears-consequences is the current termination point)
2. Governance principles exist but many are still instructions, not infrastructure
3. Provenance principle exists but enforcement is unclear
4. Decidability currently implicit in beads/artifacts, not queryable

**Risks:**
1. Edge of authority unclear - what happens when Dylan isn't the accountability sink?
2. Governance debt may be ungatable (psychosis-class failures)
3. Abstractions may not survive contact with different design values

**Done looks like:**
1. Decidability graph is first-class, queryable artifact
2. Governance principles that should be infrastructure ARE infrastructure
3. Early warning system for governance debt exists
4. Named and claimed - legible artifact that others can understand

---

## The Core Insight

What started as orchestration work — coordinating agents, tools, and workflow steps — turned out to be something more fundamental: **governance infrastructure**.

## Work Streams

**Infrastructure:** Decidability graph as artifact, principle→infrastructure audit, governance debt early warning
**Stress-testing:** Authority edges, composability, value-independence
**Detection:** Provenance enforcement, psychosis retrospective
**Direction:** Naming decision, build vs write decision

## Provenance

Emerged from conversation Jan 22, 2026. Handoff: ~/Downloads/governance-infrastructure-handoff.md

title: Implement session resume protocol (automatic handoff injection)
description:
Implement the hybrid session resume protocol designed in .kb/investigations/2026-01-11-design-session-resume-protocol.md

**Goal:** Zero cognitive load for Dylan - fresh Claude instances automatically receive prior session context via handoff injection.

**Approach:** Hybrid (automated hooks + manual fallback)
- Primary: OpenCode plugin + Claude Code hook auto-inject
- Fallback: `orch resume` CLI command for manual use
- Project-specific handoffs via .orch/session/latest symlink

**Implementation phases:**
1. Core command (`orch resume` with discovery + formatting)
2. Session end integration (update latest symlink)
3. Hook integration (OpenCode plugin + Claude Code hook)
4. Handoff format optimization (condensed for injection)

**Design:** .kb/investigations/2026-01-11-design-session-resume-protocol.md

title: Bug: Model badges not visible in dashboard without browser hard refresh
description:
After UI changes, vite dev server restart alone isn't enough - browser needs hard refresh to pick up new bundle.

Root cause: Browser cache or vite HMR not invalidating properly.

Fix options:
- Add cache busting to vite config
- Investigate vite HMR settings
- Document 'hard refresh required after vite restart' in CLAUDE.md

Current workaround: Hard refresh browser after vite restarts.

Context: Model visibility feature (orch-go-u5o9w) implemented correctly, but UI changes not visible without browser hard refresh.
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Added Cache-Control: no-store headers to vite dev server config. Browser will now fetch fresh assets instead of serving cached bundles after server restart. Requires manual browser testing to verify fix works (restart vite, refresh browser without hard refresh, confirm UI updates visible).

title: Audit and clean up stale in_progress issues across projects
description:
## Context

Many issues showing in_progress status may be stale (agent abandoned, never completed).
Cross-project daemon won't pick them up because they're not open.

## Task

1. Run `bd list --status in_progress` across all projects
2. For each in_progress issue:
   - Check if there's an active agent (orch status)
   - Check if workspace exists with recent activity
   - If stale (>24h, no workspace, no agent): reset to open or close
3. Consider adding age threshold to daemon for stale in_progress detection

## Projects to check

- orch-go
- beads
- kb-cli
- price-watch
- dotfiles
close_reason: Completed orch-go audit: closed 2, reset 9 to open. Cross-project cleanup noted in comments.

title: Dashboard follow feature doesn't track active tmux window
description:
**Root cause:** GetTmuxCwd() uses session-only target which returns first pane, not active window's pane.

**Fix:** Change pkg/tmux/follower.go:234-244 to first get active window index, then query that window.
close_reason: Fixed GetTmuxCwd to use two-step window targeting (get active window index, then query that window's cwd). Added tests for both happy path and error handling. All tests passing.

title: Add verification metrics to orch stats
description:
## Problem
Verification failures are being logged to events.jsonl (17 events so far) but orch stats doesn't surface this data. We can't see:
- Which verification gates fail most often
- Verification pass/fail rate
- Common failure patterns by skill

## Evidence
- Logging implemented: pkg/events/logger.go LogVerificationFailed()
- Events exist: grep -c verification.failed ~/.orch/events.jsonl → 17
- Stats doesn't read them: grep verification cmd/orch/stats_cmd.go → no results

## Solution
Add verification section to orch stats output:
```
📋 VERIFICATION METRICS
  Total attempts:     295
  Passed:            278 (94.2%)
  Failed:             17 (5.8%)
  
  Gates failed (top 5):
    test_evidence:    8 (47%)
    phase_complete:   4 (24%)
    git_diff:         3 (18%)
    visual_evidence:  2 (12%)
```

## Acceptance
- orch stats shows verification pass/fail rates
- Shows breakdown by gate type
- Optionally shows by skill
close_reason: Added verification metrics to orch stats showing pass/fail/bypass rates with gate-type breakdown. Tests pass. Commit 07795e75.

title: Bug: orchestrator skill spawns in workers-* tmux session instead of orchestrator session
description:
## Problem

When spawning an orchestrator via `orch spawn --opus orchestrator "..."`, the agent was placed in `workers-orch-go:14` instead of the `orchestrator` tmux session.

## Expected

Orchestrators should spawn in the `orchestrator` tmux session (separate from workers for visibility/hierarchy).

## Impact

- Orchestrator sessions mixed with worker sessions
- Harder to monitor orchestrators vs workers
- Breaks the visibility hierarchy (meta-orchestrator → orchestrator → workers)

## Provenance

Meta-orchestrator session 2026-01-14, spawned cleanup orchestrator and noticed wrong tmux session.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 0d968976 (Jan 14) - confirmed by agent investigating orch-go-skbdz

title: Epic: Protect Load-Bearing Guidance from Refactor Erosion
description:
**Problem:** Hard-won behavioral guidance (from real friction) has no protection against refactoring. When token budgets pressure skills, guidance looks like cruft and gets cut. Friction recurs.

The system has:
- Token budgets (gates on size)
- Checksums (gates on unexpected changes)
- No mechanism to distinguish 'essential insight' from 'verbose fluff'

Knowledge flows: Friction → Insight → Guidance → (time passes) → Refactor → Guidance lost → Friction recurs

**Goal:** System-level protection for guidance that came from friction. When refactoring, the question isn't 'is this verbose?' but 'what breaks if we remove this?'

**What's needed:**
1. Data model for linking friction → guidance (provenance)
2. Way to mark guidance as 'load-bearing' in skills
3. Tooling to register these links (kb friction? skillc protect?)
4. Gates that prevent silent erosion during refactors

**Why epic-worthy:**
- Affects all skills (not just orchestrator)
- Prevents recurring friction (compound value over time)
- Requires coherent design before implementation
- Multiple deliverables with dependencies

**Provenance:** 
- 2026-01-08 orchestrator session with Dylan
- Trigger: Adding 'Filter Before Presenting' and 'Surface Decision Prerequisites' to skill, asking 'how do we make sure these aren't swept away in refactors?'
- Recognized as instance of 'Track Actions, Not Just State' principle applied to guidance

**Related:**
- Principle: Track Actions, Not Just State
- Principle: Friction is Signal
- Decision: ~/.kb/decisions/2026-01-08-cli-errors-reduce-cognitive-load.md (same session)
close_reason: Load-bearing guidance system complete with provenance and refactor protection

title: Design: Data model for load-bearing guidance links
description:
**Parent:** orch-go-lv3yx

**Question:** What's the data model for linking friction → guidance?

**Options to consider:**

1. In skill.yaml - load_bearing array with pattern/provenance/evidence
2. In kb as friction records - kb friction command
3. Inline in SKILL.md - HTML comments with provenance

**Design questions:**
- Where does the canonical data live?
- How does skillc consume it?
- How do we query load-bearing patterns?

**Deliverable:** Decision record with chosen data model
close_reason: Phase: Complete - Design investigation and decision record created. Data model: load_bearing[] array in skill.yaml with pattern/provenance/evidence/severity. Ready for implementation in orch-go-lv3yx.4.

title: Skill edit: Strategic Orchestrator Model updates
description:
Update orchestrator skill to reflect Strategic Orchestrator Model decision (2026-01-07-strategic-orchestrator-model.md).

## Changes needed:

### 1. Remove 'spawn to think' patterns
- Skill Selection Guide: Remove routing of 'vague scope' → design-session, 'complex design' → architect for *understanding* work
- Keep architect for *design decisions* after understanding exists
- Remove line ~995: 'Can't figure out what prior investigation found? → Spawn to synthesize'

### 2. Synthesis as orchestrator work
- Add explicit section: 'Synthesis is Orchestrator Work'
- Define what counts as synthesis (cross-agent, orchestrator does) vs investigation (codebase exploration, worker does)

### 3. Daemon coordinates / orchestrator comprehends
- Clear statement: 'Daemon coordinates (triage:ready → spawn). Orchestrator comprehends (builds mental model, synthesizes findings).'

### 4. Triage-first workflow
- Reframe orchestrator's three jobs: COMPREHEND, TRIAGE, SYNTHESIZE (not DELEGATE)
- Default is ALWAYS daemon: create issue → label triage:ready → daemon spawns
- Manual spawn is exception requiring justification
- Strengthen anti-pattern: manual spawning when daemon could handle

## Location
orch-knowledge/skills/src/meta/orchestrator/

## Validation
- Grep for 'spawn architect' patterns - should be reframed
- 'Spawn to synthesize' pattern removed
- Triage-first is clearly the default
close_reason: Updated orchestrator skill to implement Strategic Orchestrator Model. Changed three jobs from DELEGATE/TRIAGE/SYNTHESIZE to COMPREHEND/TRIAGE/SYNTHESIZE. Added two new sections: 'Synthesis is Orchestrator Work' and 'Daemon Coordinates, Orchestrator Comprehends'. Removed spawn-to-think patterns from Skill Selection Guide. Commits: orch-knowledge 87dc01b, orch-go b5a964a3.

title: Evaluate wisps for ephemeral orchestration beads
description:
## Context
Gastown has 'wisps' - ephemeral beads that exist in the database but are NOT persisted to Git. They're burned after completion, optionally squashed to a summary.

## Problem
orch-go persists all beads to .beads/issues.jsonl. Orchestration workflows (daemon polling, patrol-style checks) create noise in the audit trail.

## Investigation Scope
1. How do wisps work in Gastown? (internal/wisp/types.go - reportedly minimal)
2. What orchestration in orch-go would benefit from ephemeral tracking?
3. How would wisps interact with bd commands? (bd list should probably hide them)
4. Tradeoff: cleaner history vs full audit trail - where's the right line?

## Reference
- Gastown gap analysis: gastown/.orch/workspace/ga-inv-gastown-gap-analysis-23jan-d2f0/investigation.md
- MEOW stack: internal/formula/types.go

title: Probe 1: Technical Feasibility - Can plugins access transcript/timing for pattern detection?
description: Build minimal plugin to test data access: Can we read session transcript? Track tool execution timing? Access existing artifacts? Measure latency. Success: <5s latency, full transcript access, can correlate tools with timing. Deliverable: investigation doc with capabilities/constraints.
close_reason: Technical feasibility confirmed: OpenCode plugins can access transcript/timing via SDK types and hooks; recommend extending existing coaching.ts plugin

title: Reduce friction for Docker Claude ↔ macOS host operations
description:
## Problem

When running Claude in Docker (for rate limit escape), host-level operations require painful copy-paste:
- launchctl commands
- make build (for macOS binary)
- Any macOS-specific tooling

## Investigation Questions

1. Should orchestrator sessions default to native Claude (not Docker)?
2. Is there a way to expose limited host commands to Docker Claude?
3. Should we document clearer guidelines for when to use Docker vs native?

## Constraint

Docker Claude cannot:
- Run launchctl (different OS)
- Build macOS binaries (wrong architecture)
- Access Docker CLI (not installed in sandbox)
